title: Convergence loop in select_with_fee is provably correct; 2-pass was a real P0 bug
description: The 2-pass strategy (pass-1: estimate fee with 1 input; pass-2: re-select with actual count) was the original design and has a concrete failure case: if pass-2 selects more notes than pass-1, the fee used (based on pass-1 count) is wrong for pass-2's input count, causing builder.build() to reject with a balance error. Concrete example: amount=40_000, notes=[20k,20k,10k,10k]. Pass-1 selects 3 notes at fee=10_000; pass-2 sees 3 inputs → fee should be 15_000, but the builder receives fee=10_000 for 3 inputs and rejects. The convergence loop fixes this by iterating until required_fee == fee, which is guaranteed to terminate (zip317_fee is monotone-non-decreasing in note count, finitely many notes). A future contributor might suggest reverting to 2-pass 'for simplicity' — it is not simpler, it is wrong.
close_reason: decision records documented in beads; no code change required
